name: The Poison-Bard
concept: You sing, you charm, you kill. In this land a bard is an entertainer, a spy, and a poisoner-for-hire, all in one bright costume — and the guild that trained you takes its cut of every performance, especially the fatal ones.
duty: The best murders look like applause. The guild taught you that, and the guild never stops being owed.

look.label: Tell
options: rings that are not just rings
you always know where the exits are

gear.label: Instrument
options: a singer's voice and a hidden blade (d6)
a set of pipes and a needle

gear.label: Craft
options: a folio of songs, codes, and secrets

gear.label: Guild-mark
options: a debt-tally the guild keeps on you
a name that opens certain doors

moves.name: Hold the Room
moves.trigger: When you perform to sway a crowd, hold their eyes, or work a gathering.

results.success: They are rapt. For as long as you hold them, the room is yours.
results.failure: You lose them, or the wrong person sees through the act.
advantage: Hold 1 Performance (spend: slip away or into somewhere unseen / plant a rumor or a coded word / lift a purse, a key, or a letter under cover of applause / doctor a cup or a dish unremarked).
You read the room's true mood and who really rules it.
threat: A heckler, a rival, or a suspicious eye disrupts the act.
Your message reaches an ear it was never meant for.
results.triumph: The whole room moves as you want it to.
results.despair: Someone in the crowd was performing back, and they are better than you.

hold.name: Performance
spend: Slip away, or into somewhere you shouldn't be, unseen.
Plant a rumor, a coded word, or a suggestion.
Lift a purse, a key, or a letter under cover of the crowd.
Doctor a cup, a dish, or a blade without anyone marking it.

moves.name: Guild Cut
moves.trigger: When you call on the guild for a contract, a poison, a safehouse, or a name (Invoke Patron, core.md):
moves.text: The guild provides, and adds 1 Debt. It collects in coin, in silence, or in blood, and it decides which. No one leaves the guild owing.

moves.name: Poisoner's Craft
moves.trigger: When you brew, choose, or apply a poison, or work out what one has done.

results.success: You have the right poison for the need, and know how to deliver it.
results.failure: You lack what you need, or misjudge the dose or the victim.
advantage: It is subtle: slow, deniable, or mistakable for illness.
threat: It leaves a trace a knowing eye could find.
You handle it carelessly and catch a touch yourself.
results.despair: It works on the wrong person, or is traced straight back to you.

moves.name: Silver Tongue
moves.trigger: When you talk someone into what you want with charm, flattery, or a beautiful lie.

results.success: They come around to your way, at least for now.
results.failure: They don't buy it, and now they are wary of you.
advantage: They do you an extra kindness.
They will not remember it was your idea.
threat: The lie has a short life; the GM says when it comes due.
results.despair: Your charm lands you a devoted enemy.

moves.name: Read the Mark
moves.trigger: When you study a person you mean to charm, rob, or kill.

results.success: Ask the GM one: what do they crave? who do they trust? when are they alone and unguarded?
results.failure: You misread them, and it will show at the worst moment.
advantage: You learn their one real weakness.
threat: Getting close enough to learn this exposed you.
results.triumph: You know them well enough to become whoever they most want to see.
results.despair: The mark was studying you the whole time.

moves.name: Killing Grace
moves.trigger: When you strike a sudden, precise blow with blade, needle, or worse, on someone who does not expect it.

results.success: The blow lands true, and quietly.
results.failure: You miss the moment, and now they know what you are.
advantage: No one else realizes what happened, yet.
threat: It is messier or louder than you wanted.
results.despair: The blow lands and does not stop them, and now they are looking right at you.

bonds: ____ has been my audience and my alibi more than once.
The guild once had me mark ____ for death, and I did not do it. I have not told them why.
____ knows a song I sang for a killing, and could hang me with it.
____ believes the performer and not the poisoner, and I let them.
I owe ____ for a secret they kept about a job gone wrong.
communityTie: The Free Legion loves the songs and doesn't ask about the rest; ____ suspects what your bright costume hides.
The guild wants eyes and a blade inside the Legion; ____ is the one you refuse to report on.
You keep the Legion's spirits up when nothing else can; ____ knows how much that performance costs you.

tracks.name: Debt
tracks.note: At 6 the guild makes a major demand, then resets to 3.

tracks.name: Heat
tracks.note: Attention from marks, rivals, and the watch (core.md).

basic: Take a move from another playbook.
Take a move from another playbook.
Increase your maximum held Performance by 1.
Master a signature poison, disguise, or song that is yours alone.
Train a move you lean on: it upgrades 2 dice to proficiency (never more than its stat).
Buy down your guild Debt by 1, permanently, with a job well done.
advanced: Raise one stat by 1 (max 5). Rare, and only at a true turning point.
Break with the guild: go independent, resolving the fallout in play.
Build a troupe: found a Bastion (a playhouse and its network of eyes) for the Legion.
